hoodType: kitchen hood and hood suppression systems
cleaningFrequencyBands: Grand Island requires a Fire Department permit workflow for kitchen hood and suppression system work, including a Kitchen Hood System Application and a Hood Suppression System application or permit record.

Grand Island requires a separate Fire Department final inspection on commercial projects before occupancy, and the hood suppression permit says installation must conform to city ordinances, the International Fire Code, and Nebraska-adopted NFPA 96.
certificateRequirement: Keep the kitchen hood or hood suppression permit paperwork, contractor details, and any final sign-off records available on site.
serviceTagRequirement: Maintain visible service documentation and contractor records that match the permitted hood or suppression work when the Fire Department inspects the project.
reportRetentionRule: Retain permit records, contractor information, and final inspection proof with the kitchen's fire-safety paperwork before the certificate of occupancy or completion is requested.
suppressionInspectionRequirement: The hood suppression permit says a final inspection must be requested and completed by the Fire Prevention Division after the system is installed or repaired.
